name: archetypes/title-hero
title: Title hero
description: The deck opener: badges, a display-size claim, a support line, and an optional figure slot. Ships a no-figure statement variant per the image-sourcing ladder.
useCases: the first slide of any deck
a major reset where the audience needs a cover-style pause

title.description: One claim, not a label — say what the deck proves.

figure.description: Follow the image ladder: real asset → authored diagram → figure --abstract → the statement variant.

motion.notes: The default entrance stagger carries the opener; do not add steps to a title slide.

icons.guidance: At most one sprite icon inside a badge; never an icon row.

useWhen: restarting attention after a long section
avoidWhen: you have no real asset and no authored diagram and the abstract art fights the style — use the statement variant
mid-deck content slides — use archetypes/statement, archetypes/section, or a content archetype instead

alternatives.when: a plain divider between chapters is enough
the opener is a bare claim with no meta

attributes.name: data-ls-archetype
attributes.description: Marks the slide for contract linting and deck scorecards.

usage: Title is a claim ("Latency fell while traffic doubled"), never a label ("Q3 update").
Use the statement variant when no honest figure exists; never fill the figure slot with text-in-a-panel.
Use ls-hero-media + ls-hero-copy for the figure variant; do not place grouped hero copy directly in aligned subgrid regions.
